meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve no design skills (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome no design skills. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Project Management to Solve No Design Skills
content.seo_description: Learn how to leverage project management and AI tools to overcome a lack of design skills. A beginner-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: You don't need a design degree to build professional SaaS products. By treating design as a structured project management task, you can use AI to generate UI components and system architectures. Focus on functional wireframing and modular design tokens to ensure your app looks clean, consistent, and highly professional.
content.intro:
The blank editor stares back at you, a haunting reminder of the chasm between your logic-driven code and the polished, aesthetic interfaces you admire on Product Hunt. You have the technical vision, but the 'design wall' feels like an insurmountable barrier that keeps your best ideas locked in your head.

But here is the secret: design is not magic; it is a system. By shifting your perspective from 'making things pretty' to 'managing the information architecture,' you can leverage AI to bridge the gap. Today, we’re tearing down that wall using Antigravity and Claude Code to turn your technical projects into high-fidelity, user-ready applications.

steps.title: Define the Information Architecture
steps.description: Before coding, map out the user journey. Use project management principles to define the core screens required for your MVP.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a structured project roadmap and information architecture document for a task management app, focusing on minimal but high-utility UI requirements.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a JSON schema for the project roadmap'

steps.title: Establish Design Tokens
steps.description: Standardize your visual language. Using tokens ensures consistency without needing to manually design every single button or margin.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a tailwind.config.js file based on a 'modern, clean, and professional' design system with a primary color palette of slate and indigo.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'apply these design tokens to the tailwind config'

steps.title: Component-First Development
steps.description: Build atomic components. Don't think about the whole page; think about the individual blocks that make up your interface.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a set of React components for a task management dashboard: Navbar, Sidebar, TaskCard, and PrimaryButton using Radix UI primitives.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create the component library structure'

steps.title: Integrate Layout Patterns
steps.description: Use proven layout patterns like the 'Sidebar-Main' layout to immediately gain a professional look and feel without custom design effort.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a responsive dashboard layout shell that utilizes whitespace effectively and follows standard SaaS UX patterns.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'scaffold the main application layout'

steps.title: Automated Quality Assurance
steps.description: Run automated checks to ensure your design tokens and components remain consistent as you add more functionality.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a script to audit the codebase for inconsistent margin usage or hardcoded colors that deviate from the design system.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'run the visual consistency audit'

pro_tips: Always start with a grayscale wireframe to force focus on layout and hierarchy before adding colors.
Use 'Design Tokens' as your single source of truth; never hardcode a hex value directly into a component.
Embrace 'Whitespace-First' design—if in doubt, increase the padding by 50%. It instantly makes any UI look more premium.
Leverage existing component libraries like Shadcn/UI to inherit professional design standards that you don't have to build from scratch.
Iterate using 'Claude Code' to refactor small snippets rather than generating entire pages to maintain granular control over your code.
content.next_steps: Once you have your MVP layout, start integrating your first real data source and deploy to Vercel to see your project live.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to build professional SaaS products without needing a design degree. Apply these steps to your next project and watch your portfolio grow. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
